2606.15888.title: NVMOS: Non-Verbal Vocalization Quality Assessment in Speech
2606.15888.paper_summary: Non-verbal vocalizations (NVs), such as laughter, sighs, and coughs, are important acoustic cues for emotion and intent. Existing speech quality assessment methods typically focus on overall naturalness, while non-verbal TTS evaluations mainly examine whether a target NV appears with the correct type and position. However, the perceptual quality of NV events themselves remains underexplored. To address this gap, we construct an NV-MOS dataset containing outputs from multiple NV-TTS systems and naturally occurring NV samples, with ratings collected from three acoustic experts on a perceptual quality scale. We further analyze audio-capable multimodal large language models such as Gemini and find clear inconsistencies between their scores and expert ratings. These results suggest that general-purpose multimodal models cannot reliably replace human judgments for NV quality assessment. We then propose NVMOS, to our knowledge the first model that can reliably predict the perceptual quality of NV events in speech. Experimental results show that, with a local NV-event focusing module, NVMOS reaches expert-level or stronger agreement with human MOS.
2606.15888.paper_summary_zh: 非語言聲音（NVs），如笑聲、嘆息和咳嗽，是情感和意圖的重要聲學線索。現有的語音質量評估方法通常專注於整體自然性，而非語言TTS評估主要檢查目標NV是否出現於正確的類型和位置。然而，NV事件本身的感知質量仍然未被充分探索。為了解決這一空白，我們構建了一個NV-MOS數據集，其中包含來自多個NV-TTS系統的輸出和自然發生的NV樣本，並從三位聲學專家那裡收集了感知質量評分。我們進一步分析了具備音頻能力的多模態大型語言模型，如Gemini，發現它們的評分與專家評分之間存在明顯的不一致性。這些結果表明，通用的多模態模型無法可靠地替代人類對NV質量評估的判斷。我們隨後提出了NVMOS，據我們所知，這是第一個能夠可靠預測語音中NV事件感知質量的模型。實驗結果顯示，通過一個專注於局部NV事件的模塊，NVMOS達到了與人類MOS的專家級或更強的協議。
2606.15888.author: Jialong Mai et.al.
2606.15888.authors: Jialong Mai, Jinxin Ji, Xiaofen Xing, Wencui Liu, Xiangmin Xu
